title: Shawken

{{Quote|When a culture is as old as ours... with more of its history forgotten than remembered, well... there have been so very many invasions over the millennia. Once the Imperial forces withdrew—hearing that your side was on the way, I suppose—they left no greater mark than the many who came before... or I think shall come after.|Santor|<ref name="SW87" />}}
'''Shawken''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] that boasted one of the most ancient known civilizations in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], dating back to the [[Pre-Republic era]]. The [[Shawkenese]] were noted for their studies of science, technology and philosophy, and over the course of several millennia created achievements such as the low [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]-piercing [[Shawken Spire]]—one of the [[Twenty Wonders of the Galaxy]]—and the universe-destroying [[Shawken Device]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], after the withdrawal of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces from Shawken, the world became an early member of the new [[Alliance of Free Planets]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Luke... I always thought that the cities of my homeworld were fine and complicated... but this is wonderful. When you air-breathers are not trying to destroy things, you are truly capable of creating wonders.|Kiro|<ref name="SW87" />}}
A [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], Shawken was comfortably habitable by [[Human/Legends|Humans]], with a varied topography that included [[Forest/Legends|forests]] and badlands. The planet was highly urbanized by the era of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], with Shawken's modern society built upon the remains of its past.<ref name="SW87" /> Shawken was located along the [[Shawken Spur]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], which connected the world to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TUF" />

==History==
{{Quote|In addition to our technology and artwork, we Shawkenese have always been revered for our studies of philosophy. According to legend, one of our greatest scientists was also a very sincere advocate of the depressing belief of nihilism. As you know, nihilists insist that once something has gone wrong there can be no correcting it. It is best to destroy everything and hope that something better comes out of a fresh start. Our ancestor believed that out of the universal armageddon of a new, artificially generated ''Big Bang'' might come a better tomorrow... luckily for all he never because quite depressed enough to use his device, and its secrets were lost with his death...|Santor|<ref name="SW87" />}}
[[File:ShawkenCity.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A city on Shawken.]]
The Shawkenese began construction on an enormous [[Skyscraper/Legends|skyscraper]] called the [[Shawken Spire]] during the [[pre-Republic era]], a process that continued for over nine millennia. The tower was destroyed in [[18,780 BBY]], by which point it touched low [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], and was later replaced with a more modern recreation. Shawken became a [[Core Founder/Legends|Core Founder]] when the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] came into being in [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]—by that point, Shawken was considered a thriving hub of science and philosophy.<ref name="TEA" /> In ancient times, one [[Shawkenese]] scientist, an avowed nihilist, created a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] that was supposedly capable of destroying every planet in the universe by artificially creating a new [[Big Bang]]. The weapon, later known simply as the [[Shawken Device]], was never used, and its secrets lost upon his death.<ref name="SW87" /> The planet supported [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] during the [[Alsakan Conflicts/Legends|Alsakan Conflicts]] of the [[Kymoodon Era]].<ref name="egw">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Shawken became renowned around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in [[10,000 BBY]], when the [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Vicendi]] selected the Shawken Spire as one of the [[Twenty Wonders of the Galaxy]].<ref name="TEA" /> By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Shawken's society was considered one of the most ancient in the galaxy. Shawken was controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] throughout the war and was believed to still be a stronghold even after the Empire's climactic [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Imperial forces pulled out before the advance of the new [[Alliance of Free Planets]]. The Alliance sent a diplomatic delegation to Shawken led by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], where he met with local [[Politics/Legends|political]] leaders who chose a diplomat named [[Santor]] to be their representative to the upcoming [[First Conference of Free Peoples]].<ref name="SW87" />

During this meeting, several of Skywalker's compatriots—the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Rik Duel/Legends|Rik Duel]], [[Dani/Legends|Dani]] and [[Chihdo/Legends|Chihdo]]—accidentally managed to get themselves trapped while exploring some of Shawken's ancient catacombs. In doing so, they inadvertently reactivated the chambers containing the Shawken Device, knowledge of which had been passed down through the generations. Although the mechanism to deactivate the universe-destroying weapon were hidden beneath an underground river, the aquatic [[Iskalonian]] Alliance agent [[Kiro]] proved up to the task, although he suffered severe injuries in the process.<ref name="SW87" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|I can't take credit for Shawken or any of its marvels, Kiro. It's one of the oldest, most magnificent civilizations in the known galaxy.|Santor|<ref name="SW87" />}}
Shawken was inhabited by the Shawkenese, a primarily Human culture that dated back to the pre-Republic era and was considered one of the galaxy's oldest and grandest. At one time, Shawken was known as the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] heroes. The ancient Shawkenese were noted for their advanced energy technology, somehow managing to imbue seemingly inert objects with power and using them as energy sources—just how these technologies worked was a secret long lost by the time of the Galactic Civil War. In the wake of the Empire's retreat from Shawken during the Galactic Civil War, the world was governed by a council that chose Santor as its representative to the Alliance of Free Planets

==Locations==
{{Quote|There, for example, they unearthed old catacombs... we no longer know what they were once for, they are so old. They are very interesting and beautiful.|Santor|<ref name="SW87" />}}
Perhaps the most famous individual structure on the planet was the Shawken Spire, which reached beyond Shawken's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] by its destruction, and later earned status as one of the Twenty Wonders of the Galaxy. The original Shawken Spire was later replicated with more modern materials.<ref name="TEA" /> The ancient Shawkenese left behind extensive ruins and catacomb systems that were partially excavated by the Galactic Empire, and that contained mysterious treasures.<ref name="SW87" />

==Behind the scenes==
Shawken first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 87|''Star Wars'' 87]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]], illustrated by [[Tom Palmer]] and released in [[1984]].<ref name="SW87" />
